statement: Every sequence from finite augmented graph spaces with uniformly bounded graph energy has a weakly convergent graph-form subsequence in V, and closed graph forms are weakly lower semicontinuous along that subsequence.

coreDensityInputStatus.label: core density input
coreDensityInputStatus.reason: The finite sections live in the dense smooth/Galerkin core of V.

greenClosureInputStatus.label: completed Hilbert graph space input
greenClosureInputStatus.reason: The Green closure theorem supplies the completed Hilbert graph norm and continuous feature forms.

moscoLiminfStatus.label: Mosco liminf compactness
moscoLiminfStatus.reason: Bounded graph-energy sequences are bounded in the Hilbert space V; reflexivity gives weak subsequential limits and closed convex quadratic forms are lower semicontinuous.

proof: Use the completed graph norm from the Green closure theorem.
Bounded repaired/graph energy gives boundedness in Hilbert space V.
Use Hilbert reflexivity for weak subsequential compactness.
Use closed convex quadratic-form lower semicontinuity for liminf.
